content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 22, 1998


Kurt Hahn
Finance/Economic Services Director
City of Healdsburg
401 Grove Street
Healdsburg, California 95448-4723

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-98-126

Dear Mr. Hahn:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Does your prospective membership on a nonprofit corporation board of directors create a conflict of interest because of your position as Finance/Economic Services Director for the City of Healdsburg?

CONCLUSION

A conflict of interest under the Act would not exist based on the facts presented if you were to become a member of a nonprofit corporation and also serve in your position as Finance/Economic Services Director for the City of Healdsburg. The Commission only has jurisdiction over the Political Reform Act. You may want to contact the Attorney General's Office regarding any issues pertaining to Government Code Section 1090.


___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-98-126
Page 2

FACTS

You are the Finance/Economic Services Director for the City of Healdsburg (the "city")
and a potential member on the board of directors of a new nonprofit corporation which may bid
for, acquire and operate Healdsburg Community Hospital (the "hospital"). Board members of the
new nonprofit corporation will not be compensated.

The hospital is currently owned by HCA/Columbia. You have no interest in any form in
HCA/Columbia. The new nonprofit corporation may seek conduit tax exempt bond financing
through the Healdsburg Public Improvement Corporation ("HPIC") which has an independent
board, but is a component unit of the city and whose actions must be affirmed by the city council.
In a conduit bond issue there are certain ministerial actions the City Finance/Economic Services
Director/Assistant Treasurer is obligated to take, primarily signing trust agreements and other
documents authorized by HPIC and city resolutions. Conduit bonds neither impact city finances
nor impose a financial risk. They also do not create financial gain or loss for the city or HPIC.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official from making, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in
which the official has a financial interest. Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the official or a
member of his or her immediate family or on (in pertinent part):

"(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans
by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of
business on terms available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in
value provided to, received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director,
officer, partner, trustee, employee or holds any position of
management." (Section 87103(c) and (d), emphasis added.)

The definition of "business entity" as used above means any organization or enterprise
operated for profit. (Section 82005.) Accordingly, since the new nonprofit corporation is not
operated for profit, it is not the type of business entity covered by Section 87103(d). Therefore,
your potential position on the board of directors of a new nonprofit corporation would not result
in a financial interest to you within the meaning of Section 87103(d). In addition, because you
will not be compensated by the nonprofit corporation, it would not be a disqualifying source of
income under Section 87103(c).

File No. A-98-126
Page 3

However, the Commission only has jurisdiction over the Political Reform Act. You may want to contact the Attorney General's Office regarding any issues pertaining to Government Code Section 1090.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Lynda Doherty
Political Reform Consultant, Legal Division
